definitions: An ancient Greek philosopher born in Elea, Magna Graecia.
He was the founder of the Eleatic school of philosophy.
His only known work is a poem, 'On Nature', of which only fragments survive.
examples: Parmenides argued that reality is a single, unchanging, and eternal being.
The ideas of Parmenides significantly influenced Plato.
Scholars continue to debate the interpretation of Parmenides's philosophy.
